onboarding.description: Optional readiness: complete flag, wizard metadata, required_integrations checked by validate-config.

required_integrations.description: Logical ids: jira, slack, notion, github, gcal, sheets, gbrain_team, gbrain_personal.

team.description: Team identity, roster, business hours, canonical group — see config-schema.md

sprint.description: Sprint cadence, ceremonies, capacity metric, velocity window.

approval_chains.description: Template id and named approval chains (string[] per action).

knowledge_base.description: Local roots, globs, doc URLs, GitHub scope, retrieval prompt, optional gbrain include.

knowledge_storage.description: Disk fallback and team/personal Git remotes for persisted knowledge.

integrations.description: jira, slack, notion, github, gcal, sheets, share_html, google_drive, transcripts — see config-schema.md

mcp_servers.description: MCP server registry; values often { command, args, env } — see integration-guide.md

gbrain.description: Team vs personal GBrain base URLs, trust_policy, provenance envelope for writes.

session.description: default_gbrain_target, session id, auto_end, metrics, diary prompt.

default_gbrain_target.description: Typically team or personal — routes GBrain writes.

notion_defaults.description: Notion routing: parent_pages, post_targets, private_vault, team_notion (incl. teamspace_anchor_page_id + setup_complete), database_ids, template_pages, and the template catalog system (template_set + template_catalog_path).

template_set.description: Active template catalog set id (e.g. 'official', 'alternative_sentele', or a custom id). Resolved against templates/notion/catalog/<id>.json.

template_catalog_path.description: Plugin-relative directory containing catalog set JSON files. Defaults to 'templates/notion/catalog'.

surface_routing.description: Maps a logical surface (team_hub | private_vault | one_on_ones) to the config block that owns it (team_notion | private_vault). Skills use this to know which root to land pages under.

teamspace_anchor_page_id.description: Required for team_hub creation: the Notion API has no teamspace_id parent type. The user creates one empty page inside the target teamspace via Notion UI and pastes its id here. jstack-notion-setup uses this id as the parent for the team-side tree.

parent_pages.description: Page id per logical key. Keys include: team_hub, private_root, one_on_ones, pe_index, team_people, team_metrics, team_notes, product_strategy, product_wiki, knowledge_base, sops, ops_dashboard, private_scratchpad, private_notes, private_data, private_transcripts, private_projects, private_sprints. Empty string means not configured.

database_ids.description: Database / data source ids. Convention: '<key>' for the database id, '<key>_data_source' for the collection id (used by query/update tools). Common keys: tasks, calendar, intake, contacts, documents, product_tasks, projects, performance_reviews.

template_pages.description: Runtime template page ids — duplicated by the matching skill (e.g. /jstack:notion standup duplicates template_pages.standup per standup). Keys: standup, team_report, performance, one_on_one, meeting_notes, plus any catalog-defined page_template entries.

golden_pages.description: Setup-time golden source pages. Convention: key = catalog template id (e.g. 'kanban', 'company_team_page'). When non-empty, jstack-notion-setup duplicates from that page instead of creating from the catalog's markdown/SQL — preserves visually-rich layouts (configured DB views, embedded blocks, gallery layouts) that the API can't easily reproduce. Empty value falls back to the catalog content_path / schema.

post_targets.description: Maps an artifact kind (scratchpad, transcript, standup, etc.) to a parent_pages key. Resolves at posting time.

one_on_one_cycle.description: 1:1 prepare/after from transcripts; Lattice vs Notion; see meetings/one-on-one-transcript/references/config-shape.md

debug.description: Verbose traces and optional MCP test fixtures. Use debug.enabled as the canonical boolean for debug mode (not a top-level debug: true alias).

enabled.description: Canonical debug/trace toggle for tooling and future logging hooks.

mock_mcp.description: When true, operators expect the jstack MCP mock registered in .mcp.json; jstack doctor warns if wiring or scenario is missing.

mock_mcp_scenario.description: Scenario id (subdirectory under mcp-mock/scenarios, default when empty). Sets JSTACK_MCP_SCENARIO when adding jstack-mock preset.

skills.description: Per-skill overrides plus optional machine-readable output policy for CLI/MCP/automation — see skills/_core/references/output-formats.md

machine_readable.description: Policy for --output=json|yaml style responses; hosts should read this before auto-injecting structured-output flags.

enabled.description: When false, wrappers must not auto-append --output=json or --output=yaml; users may still request JSON in natural language.

require_schema_ref.description: When true, structured payloads should include a top-level $schema URI matching the skill schema file $id (strict pipelines).

distribution.description: update_check, version_url, github core/gusto repos, plugin_pr deny globs

pe.description: Performance engineering / reporting window — see defaults.json

reports.description: Report branding colors, typography — see defaults.json

cross_plugins.description: gstack, superpowers, gbrain plugin skill lists; gbrain.enabled requires non-empty skills[]

routines.description: standup, weekly_digest, sprint_close, health_check — each: enabled, cron, chain (skill ids)

evals.description: Eval gates: auto_gate, token_budgets, latency_thresholds_ms, required_fields

team_context.description: Optional team-owned markdown slices (paths only); see config-schema.md

levels_and_expectations.description: IC/EM ladder file or canonical URL; user-maintained

org_context.description: Optional org handbook, ethics, HR-safe docs, coaching, review rubrics; local paths, Notion/Drive ids, MCP hints — see skills/_core/references/org-context.md

kickoff_workflows.description: Executable morning-kickoff: morning.path, state_path, optional definitions[]
